Table DCS_ORDERHISTORY

Card of table DCS_ORDERHISTORY

Name

DCS_ORDERHISTORY

Code

DCS_ORDERHISTORY

DBMS

Snowflake


Description of the table DCS_ORDERHISTORY

---------------------- Pseudocode ----------------------------------

 

 

 

 

1. Initialize a new table in the Presentation Layer in "DB_CONS_PL_(env)" under "PL_CONSUMERORDER" schema

called "DCS_ORDERHISTORY".

 

2. Define the Columns, based on the script provided.

3. Define the Constraints

Primary Key (Unique Record Identifier):

DCS_ORDERHISTORY_KEY = MD5(Concatenation of(ORDER_ID, STATUS_TO, DATE_TIME, MARKET_CODE))

 

 

4. Save the table definition.

 

5. Maintain Data Dictionary

i. Add the column description to each column (from Comment tab in PD).

ii. Mention the Data Classification & PII category for each column and mask them as per standard guideline.

 

6. The data in the target object must stay deduplicated.

 

7. Verify whether the newcoming ingested records in the source already exist in the Presentation Layer with any previous load, based on the

Unique Record Identifier.

i. If Yes, don't propagate them to the Presentation Layer.

ii. If No, propagate them to the Presentation Layer.

 

 

8. Follow the Entity & Attribute level Business Rules defined.

 

10. Run the RLS specific script based on the MARKET_CODE (applicable for QAS & Prod).

 

 

 

 

 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-------------------------------------- Load Type -----------------------------------------------------------------

APPEND

 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 

 


Check constraint name of the table DCS_ORDERHISTORY

CKT_DCS_ORDERHISTORY


Server validation rule of the table DCS_ORDERHISTORY

%RULES%


Code preview of the table DCS_ORDERHISTORY

drop table DCS_ORDERHISTORY cascade;

--==============================================================
-- Table: DCS_ORDERHISTORY
--==============================================================
create or replace table PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY (
DCS_ORDERHISTORY_KEY TEXT not null,
ORDER_ID INTEGER not null,
DCS_ORDER_KEY TEXT not null,
STATUS_TO TEXT not null,
TITLE TEXT,
DATE_TIME DATE not null,
MARKET_CODE TEXT not null,
constraint PK_DCS_ORDERHISTORY primary key (DCS_ORDERHISTORY_KEY)
not enforced
);

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY.DCS_ORDERHISTORY_KEY is
'Primary Key. Unique record identifier. ';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY.ORDER_ID is
'Order Identifier. ';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY.DCS_ORDER_KEY is
'Primary Key. Unique record identifier.';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY.DATE_TIME is
'Timestamp when order changed status';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY.MARKET_CODE is
'Identifier for the Market -> 2 letter Country Code + ''04''';

alter table PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY
add constraint FK_DCS_ORDE_R1_ORDER__DCS_ORDE foreign key (ORDER_ID)
references PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDER (DCS_ORDER_KEY)
not enforced not deferrable;

alter table PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDERHISTORY
add constraint FK_DCS_ORDE_R22_ORDER_DCS_ORDE foreign key (DCS_ORDER_KEY)
references PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDER (DCS_ORDER_KEY)
not enforced not deferrable;
